project_privacy_data_flows.description: During onboarding, the app requests location permissions (ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION, ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION, ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOCATION) from the user. This flow only covers the permission grant/deny interaction; actual location data collection begins later via LocationSensorManager.

flow_steps.details: User taps 'Share Location' button during onboarding. The app prepares to request location permissions.
System location permission dialog is shown to the user, requesting ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION, ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION, and ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOCATION (Android Q+).

flow_steps.code: sensorRepository.setSensorsEnabled(sensorIds = [backgroundLocation.id, zoneLocation.id, singleAccurateLocation.id], serverId = serverId, enabled = enabled)
flow_steps.details: Location sensor enable state is written to the local Room database (Sensor table). Permission result is recorded; denial is accepted without blocking onboarding.

project_privacy_data_flows.data_type: Location Permission Status (for local network detection)
project_privacy_data_flows.description: During onboarding, if the server connection uses HTTP, the app requests location permission to detect whether the device is on the home Wi-Fi network for secure connection configuration.

flow_steps.details: User selects 'Most Secure' option and taps save. Location permission is requested for home network detection.

System location permission dialog is shown. If granted, the app allows insecure connection setting and navigates to home network setup. If denied, a snackbar error is shown and the option reverts to 'Less Secure'.
